The '''''Dianoga''-class assault starfighter''' was engineered by [[Koensayr Manufacturing/Legends|Koensayr Manufacturing]] and saw use in the years prior and during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==History==
[[File:Dianoga_swsf.jpg|thumb|left|160px|A head-on view of a ''Dianoga''.]]
Created in the waning days of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the ''Dianoga'' was a bulky [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] and extraordinarily overpowered for a peacetime vessel. Growing chaos in the Republic, however, ensured that few questioned the purpose of such a powerful fighter.

The ''Dianoga'' soon became popular with [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] groups, as it was comparatively cheap and easy to obtain in comparison to military-grade fighters. This would soon result, after the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], however, since the line was discontinued it was hard to find parts to maintain them.<ref name="TCWCG" />

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] happens in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> mercenaries contracted by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] launched an [[Attempted kidnapping of Queen Amidala|attack]] against [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The survivors later [[Skirmish at Eos|tangled]] with [[Vana Sage]], another mercenary who had intercepted their transmission.<ref name="SW:SF">[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Dianoga starfighters appear in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] and in the game's [[2002]] sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''.
